description: Deterministic validation contract for NCC XML source ingestion with candidate-backed semantic promotion.

representation_model.alignment_dependency: XML must pass its gate before XML-to-PDF alignment and downstream candidate extraction can be trusted.

status_model.PASS: All blocking rules pass and no review-or-warning outcomes are triggered.
status_model.PASS_WITH_WARNINGS: All blocking rules pass and one or more warning outcomes are triggered.
status_model.REVIEW_REQUIRED: No blocking rule is triggered, but one or more review-required outcomes must be resolved before alignment progression.
status_model.FAIL: Validation completed and the source did not satisfy contract requirements.
status_model.BLOCKED: A blocking rule prevents progression to alignment, candidate, and later semantic stages.

rules.description: XML must parse successfully, use valid encoding, and expose an expected NCC root element.
rules.pass_when: xml.is_well_formed == true AND xml.encoding_valid == true AND xml.root_element in thresholds.expected_root_elements
rules.block_when: xml.is_well_formed != true OR xml.encoding_valid != true OR xml.root_element not in thresholds.expected_root_elements

rules.description: XML must expose edition, amendment or version, volume, and section or part context where applicable.
rules.pass_when: xml.metadata.edition != null AND xml.metadata.amendment != null AND xml.metadata.volume != null AND (xml.metadata.section != null OR xml.metadata.part != null OR xml.context_not_applicable == true)
rules.block_when: xml.metadata.edition == null OR xml.metadata.amendment == null OR xml.metadata.volume == null OR ((xml.metadata.section == null AND xml.metadata.part == null) AND xml.context_not_applicable != true)

rules.description: XML hierarchy must preserve valid NCC nesting and contain no invalid parent-child links, impossible nesting, or orphaned structural nodes.
Addressable clause, table, figure, definition, and node identifiers must be unique and stable.
Required structural nodes must contain usable content, including non-empty headings, clause bodies, and table containers unless explicitly allowed to be empty.
Internal references must resolve to existing XML targets when required. Small bounded unresolved sets may pass with warnings.
Defined terms must remain structurally identifiable and linkable from referencing clauses where available. Small bounded failures may pass with warnings.
XML table structures must preserve enough row, header, and cell-grouping information for downstream interpretation and later PDF/XML relationship checks.

XML must be suitable for candidate extraction and later candidate-backed snippet promotion by preserving stable clause identity, hierarchy, node content, references, and traceability metadata needed for later PDF relationships.
Overall XML quality score must meet the configured minimum threshold.

candidate_stage_contract.entry_requirement: Validated XML may participate in candidate extraction only after alignment prerequisites are satisfied.
candidate_stage_contract.snippet_promotion_rule: Canonical snippets must be promoted from validated candidates rather than generated directly from XML structure.
candidate_stage_contract.compatibility_note: The legacy rule identifier X9_SNIPPET_READINESS is retained for compatibility while candidate-stage semantics become normative.
